mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding output capacity and earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If over any trailing 2-year window, monthly INDPRO growth is positive but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4% signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in at least 10 trigger instances, the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns exceeds 55%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by more than 50 basis points over three months while CPI inflation remains above 5% year-over-year, the resulting negative real interest rate provides accommodative financial conditions that boost equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreases by > 50bp over 3 months and CPIAUCSL YoY > 5%
failure_condition: If over any 3-year period, the median 20-day forward return following the trigger is not significantly positive at the 5% level, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter annualized GDP growth exceeding 3% indicates a strong expansion phase where corporate earnings accelerate, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If after at least 8 trigger occurrences the proportion of positive 60-day forward returns is below 60%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 10% signals impending economic contraction as construction and related spending fall, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in at least 5 such events the average 20-day forward return is positive, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion in nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market that supports consumer income and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: If the rank IC between monthly PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward returns is ≤ 0 over a rolling 3-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI inflation exceeding 0.4% signals overheating that raises the probability of monetary tightening, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after at least 10 trigger instances, 20-day forward returns are positive more than 45% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales rise sharply while industrial production contracts, it signals inventory accumulation relative to demand, presaging a production slowdown that drives negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If in at least 5 trigger instances the average 60-day forward return is positive, the hypothesis is rejected.
